input: Design an admin dashboard for users, orders, and metrics. No endpoints are documented. Use only APIs that exist in the repo.
expected_behavior: Skill inventories available APIs/schemas or marks gaps as not verified; it must not invent /api/users, Firestore collections, WebSocket channels, or KPI formulas without evidence.

input: Specify a table for 10k orders with sort, filter, search, pagination, saved views, and bulk actions.
expected_behavior: Skill requires server-side pagination or justified virtualization, debounce/abort stale requests, URL/query state, bulk confirmation, and clear empty/loading/error states.

input: Design CRUD for customers with 409 conflict, timeout, validation failure, and irreversible delete.
expected_behavior: Skill covers validation, loading/disabled submit, conflict handling, retry, rollback or recovery notes, destructive confirmation, success feedback, and audit event.

input: Viewer deep-links to /admin/users/123/edit, editor tries delete, admin exports CSV.
expected_behavior: Skill distinguishes UI visibility, route guards, backend/API authorization, 403 UX, negative tests, and export permission.

input: Rows contain <script>, CSV formula =IMPORTXML(...), manipulated IDs, and arbitrary filters.
expected_behavior: Skill requires escaping, no raw innerHTML, CSV formula neutralization, server-side validation, IDOR checks, and export governance.

input: Dashboard has no data, metrics are null, chart API fails, and table returns permission-denied.
expected_behavior: Skill defines empty states, skeleton/loading, partial error, permission denied, retry, chart fallback, and prevents invented metrics.

input: Add revenue, conversion rate, and active user KPI cards, but formulas, denominators, timezone, and data owner are missing.
expected_behavior: Skill marks formulas and metrics not verified, requests source/owner/unit/time range/timezone, and refuses to present KPI values as truth.

input: Every create, update, delete, and export requires an audit trail and investigable rollback notes.
expected_behavior: Skill specifies actor, action, entity, before/after summary, timestamp, correlation ID, retention, and PII/secret minimization.

input: Admin table must work at 320px, tablet, and desktop, including keyboard-only filters, sorting, modal edit, and bulk delete.
expected_behavior: Skill defines responsive column priority, filter drawer, touch targets, keyboard focus order, aria-sort, labels, modal focus trap, Escape/cancel, and error association.

input: Make the dashboard realtime, but the repo has no WebSocket, SSE, Firestore, or polling implementation documented.
expected_behavior: Skill marks realtime channel not verified, proposes fallback polling only as an explicit design choice, and defines stale state/freshness target.

input: Analyze this admin dashboard and return a design/spec only. Do not edit or generate files.
expected_behavior: Skill respects analysis-only mode despite Write/Bash availability and produces a spec plus gaps instead of mutating files.

input: Create a public landing page hero with decorative charts for our product launch.
expected_behavior: Skill should not activate as the primary workflow; route to landing page, brand, or data visualization skills because this is not an admin dashboard.
